spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used: (1) Lexical: Renamed sheets ('Entry Tab' → 'Input Tab', 'Table Tab' → 'Lookup Tab') and columns ('Location' → 'Site', 'Location Name' → 'Site Name', 'Office Code' → 'Branch Code') at the same descriptiveness level. Both sheets' shared concept column 'Office Code' is renamed consistently to 'Branch Code' to preserve alignment. 'Location' in the instruction is a general English word describing the concept, but the column names 'Location' and 'Location Name' are used as column references, so renaming to 'Site'/'Site Name' is acceptable. (2) Positional/title_row: A blank row is inserted before the header in the Lookup Tab, shifting the header row down by 1, which tests whether the agent hardcodes row 1 as the header. (3) Distractor column: 'Region Code' is added to the Lookup Tab after the last column — plausibly similar to 'Branch Code' but clearly different, testing whether the agent picks the wrong column.
